author: gemini
review_notes: Excellent updates. All critical issues have been successfully addressed.
Tape vs Disk SCSI Parsing: `READ (6)` and `WRITE (6)` are now correctly decoded as SSC commands using `read_write_6_transfer_blocks`. Variable vs Fixed block transfers are handled correctly.
Performance: `apply_mutation` was rewritten to group SHA256 hashes by blocks of 32 bytes using a counter, eliminating the per-byte hashing overhead.
Performance: SQLite connections are now persistent inside `FaultEngineInner`, resolving the I/O bottleneck.

Position Tracking: A robust `PositionUpdate` state machine was introduced. It correctly handles `SPACE (6)`, `SPACE (16)`, `LOCATE (16)`, `WRITE FILEMARKS`, and `READ POSITION` to keep `current_lba` tightly synchronized with the device.

Note on 16-byte I/O commands: My previous review note regarding missing `READ (16)` and `WRITE (16)` commands was incorrect, as those do not exist in the SSC specification (tapes exclusively use the 6-byte I/O variants for data transfer). The implementation correctly ignored that flawed advice and appropriately implemented the 16-byte commands that *do* exist in SSC (e.g., `SPACE (16)` and `LOCATE (16)`).
